title: Roku Q1 2026 Earnings Report
text: Roku exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q1 2026, reporting total revenue of $1.248 billion, representing a 22% year-over-year increase. The company swung to profitability with net income of $85.7 million (57 cents per diluted share), compared to a net loss of $27.4 million in the prior-year period. Adjusted EBITDA surged 165% to $148.4 million, and shares jumped more than 11% in after-hours trading. The Platform segment generated $1.13 billion in revenue, growing 28% with a gross margin of 51.6%. Advertising revenue climbed 27% to $613 million. Company leaders stated that "video advertising growth on the Roku platform outpaced both the U.S. over-the-top streaming and digital ad markets," attributing this to their combination of scale, data, and ad technology. Subscriptions revenue grew 30% to $519 million (23% excluding the acquired Frndly service), with Q1 marking the company's highest quarter for premium subscription sign-ups. Management raised full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance to $675 million and projects Platform revenue growth of nearly 21% to $5.0 billion for 2026. For Q2, the company expects Platform revenue growth of approximately 20%.
